data.title: BNB Surpasses 910 USDT with a 1.65% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Dec 04, 2025, 14:33 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 910 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 910.059998 USDT, with a narrowed 1.65% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: AlphaTON Capital Files $420.69 Million Shelf Registration to Boost AI and HPC Expansion
data.description:
According to Foresight News, AlphaTON Capital, a Nasdaq-listed company, has submitted a shelf registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for $420.69 million. This move aims to enhance the company's financing flexibility to support future expansion projects in artificial intelligence and high-performance computing infrastructure.

The shelf registration statement, authorized under U.S. securities law, allows issuers to offer securities over a specified period, typically three years, in multiple rounds. This method enables companies to strategically time their offerings based on market conditions and funding needs.

In addition to infrastructure expansion, AlphaTON Capital plans to pursue strategic mergers and acquisitions within profitable companies in the Telegram ecosystem.

data.title: Android Chip Vulnerability Poses Risk to Web3 Wallets, Researchers Warn
data.description:
According to Foresight News, researchers at Ledger have identified a vulnerability in Android chips that could lead to devices being fully controlled, posing a risk to smartphone-based Web3 wallets from physical attacks. The Donjon research team at Ledger discovered that hardware fault injection can bypass core security checks and take complete control of the chip. The company emphasized that this finding does not affect Ledger hardware wallets but highlights the risks of relying solely on smartphone hot wallets for digital asset protection.

The Donjon team detailed in their report how they detected the vulnerability in MediaTek's Dimensity 7300 (MT6878) chip, which is widely used in many Android consumer phones. Ledger stated that the research confirms that even the most advanced smartphone chips remain susceptible to physical attacks, making them unsuitable for safeguarding private keys. The vulnerability was disclosed to MediaTek in May. Ledger noted that MediaTek has responded positively and has informed affected manufacturers.

data.title: Cloudflare just stopped the largest DDoS attack ever
data.description:
Cloudflare announced that it had detected and stopped the 29. 7-terabit-per-second (Tbps) distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack, which was the biggest one ever. According to the web infrastructure and security company, the activity originated from a DDoS botnet-for-hire known as AISURU. It has been linked to several hyper-volumetric DDoS attacks over the past year. The attack lasted […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/cloudflare-stop-largest-ddos-attack/

data.title: Digital Asset Holdings Secures $50 Million in Latest Funding Round
data.description:
According to Foresight News, blockchain company Digital Asset Holdings LLC has successfully raised $50 million in a new funding round. The investment was backed by prominent financial institutions including Bank of New York Mellon Corp., Nasdaq Inc., S&P Global, and iCapital. Based in New York, Digital Asset Holdings is known for developing the public blockchain Canton Network.

This latest funding follows an earlier round this year where the company raised $135 million. The previous round was led by DRW Venture Capital and Tradeweb Markets, with participation from market makers such as Citadel Securities, IMC, and Optiver.

data.title: Riot Platforms Reports November 2025 Bitcoin Mining Output
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Riot Platforms, a company listed on Nasdaq, has released its unaudited production and operations update for November 2025. The report reveals that the company mined 428 BTC during the month, averaging a daily output of 14.3 BTC. Additionally, Riot Platforms sold 383 BTC in November, increasing its total Bitcoin holdings to 19,368 BTC.

data.title: Security Vulnerability Found in Mediatek Smartphone Chip
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Ledger's Donjon research team has demonstrated that electromagnetic fault injection (EMFI) can completely compromise a commonly used Mediatek smartphone chip, which is found in many Android phone models. This issue requires attackers to have physical access to the device, highlighting the risks faced by users storing private keys on smartphones.

Ledger stated that its team examined the Mediatek Dimensity 7300 (MT6878) chip produced by TSMC. Researchers used EMFI tools to disrupt the chip's boot ROM, successfully bypassing core security checks and gaining full control of the chip, allowing arbitrary code execution at the highest privilege level (EL3). Ledger emphasized that this discovery does not affect Ledger hardware wallets.

Ledger disclosed this vulnerability to Mediatek in May. Mediatek responded by stating that EMFI attacks are beyond the security scope of the MT6878 chip, which is designed for consumer products rather than financial or hardware security module applications. Mediatek also noted that devices with higher security requirements, such as encrypted hardware wallets, should include specialized defense measures.

The report comes at a time when physical attacks targeting cryptocurrency users are on the rise globally.

data.title: CertiK Report Highlights U.S. Digital Asset Regulatory Framework by 2025
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Web3 security firm CertiK has released the '2025 Skynet U.S. Digital Asset Policy Report,' which outlines the trajectory of digital asset regulation in the United States by 2025. The report indicates that the U.S. has established a relatively clear and systematic regulatory framework for digital assets, focusing on key areas such as issuance, trading, and custody. Federal legislative and administrative reforms are converging, transitioning the market from an 'exploration phase' to a 'systematic stage.'

As regulatory directions for centralized functions become increasingly defined, digital assets are steadily integrating into the U.S. financial system. Financial institutions are shifting their strategic focus towards licensed digital assets, with code auditing becoming a crucial method for ensuring institutional security and compliance. This trend is driving the ongoing development of the industry.
